summary: A vibrant March‑1973 Utica show captured on a solid soundboard tape – stellar performances of Here Comes Sunshine, Dark Star > Eyes and Bertha shine despite occasional hum and vocal clipping.
review: The recording delivers a clear, mostly well‑balanced soundboard mix that lets the band’s chemistry sparkle, even though a faint hum and some vocal oversaturation are noted. Musically the show is a highlight of the spring ’73 tour, with Jerry’s harmonies on “Me & Bobby McGee,” Bob’s soaring lead on “Here Comes Sunshine,” and a soaring Dark Star‑>Eyes sequence that many reviewers call exceptional.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is 4.5; the vast majority praise the performance and deem the recording good despite a few technical flaws, yielding an overall strong score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced soundboard tape; minor hum, occasional vocal clipping/oversaturation, and the first few seconds of “Bertha” missing for some listeners.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show opens with a powerful “Bertha,” flows through jazzy, melodic first‑set material and builds into an expansive second set where Dark Star, Eyes of the World and other jams dominate; audience interaction is captured nicely, giving the concert a lively, organic feel.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for beautiful playing, harmonizing on “Me & Bobby McGee,” and noted for following Keith with a Slipknot‑riff doodle.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned in banter about St. Stephen and Take a Step Back; no specific musical praise recorded.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for stunning lead guitar during “Here Comes Sunshine,” louder in the mix and delivering great stuff.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith noted for Slipknot‑riff doodle and contributes to the jam energy; keyboards generally appreciated.
